Clemente ([2DSW4B7E]: 122) reports the monastery of Brag dkar rta so to have been founded by Rin chen rnam rgyal (1473-1557).

name: gnas rnying
alternative_names:
gnas snying
gnam snying
gnam rnying
skyegs gnas rnying
skye gnas
comments:
Place in eastern gTsang in the area of sKyeg in Nyang stod, south of rGyal rtse (ID:6454). 
The gNas rning temple was founded by rGya 'Jam dpal gsang ba (ca. 760-859) under the patronage of the mGos clan ([4LDAV8UH]: 82 and 86). It remained structurally unchanged from the 9th c. to 1045, when it became a bKa' gdams pa center ([4LDAV8UH]: 90)
For the history of the lineages, see [4LDAV8UH].
Monasteries composing the gNas rnying stong skor after the Hor invasion are listed in [Y2ZN5C6X] (cited in [4LDAV8UH]: 103): "dge ba bshes gnyen Kan logs pa, Rgya ston pa, Zla ba, Ston shag, Zla grags kyi dben pa, Gyan sa pa, Ye shes dpal, Rin 'od, Ston nam, Chos sgrub, and Sher 'bum. Moreover, Men rgan pa, Ser 'phreng pa, La drug pa, Byang mo khung pa, Drug ma ba, Lha khang pa, Mkhar ra lung pa, Grags shongs pa, Dge sdings pa, Dgon khra, 'Phang thang, Phro sdings, Sypi bsdus, Ser sdings, Dga' ldan, Dben tsha, Grum chung, G.ya' dmar ra, Rkyang po, Chu bzang, 'Bras mo, She mgur, Wa lung, Grog ra, Rmog pa, Ze thang, 'Chad mangs, Byangs mang, Bra than, Bye mda', Rtsid Gnas rnying, Nyan tsho, Stag lung, and Lho Dol."

name: gsang mda' grong
alternative_names: gsang mda' sku 'bum lha khang
comments: At the junction of the rivers gSang mda' and sKyi chu, two hours from gSang phu valley ([BAQ9MH4T]: 201, citing Chan 1994).

name: gsang phu gling smad
comments:
Lower branch of gSang phu monastery (ID:6173) resulting from a split in the late 1100s. 
Argued to be the seat of Phya pa (ID:3831)'s line ([CCSBSVCA]: 685).
[9BEDJZXI]: 121-22 (phal cher 'jam dpal seng ge'i dus su gdan sa gling stod smad gnyis su gyes zer | gling smad lo tsA'i khri dang gling stod phya pa'i khri yin zer yang | gling stod rgnog legs pa'i shes rab dang | gling smad du rngog blo ldan shes rab kyi khri yin la | gling stod smad du gyes pa'i rten 'brel yang byams 'jam dbyangs gnyis bgro gleng mdzad pa'i don gyis yin pas | grub mtha' smra ba gnyis 'byung bar lung bstan pas | sa dge gnyis dang | ra ba stod smad gnyis dang | gling stod smad ste gnyis mtshan gsum po de la 'gal ba med de | bstan pa dar ba la bzang 'gran du song ba yin no).

name: gsang phu gling stod
comments:
Upper branch of gSang phu monastery (ID:6173) resulting from a split in the late 1100s. 
Argued to be the seat of rNgog Legs pa'i shes rab (ID:3788)'s line ([CCSBSVCA]: 685).

[9BEDJZXI]: 121-22 (phal cher 'jam dpal seng ge'i dus su gdan sa gling stod smad gnyis su gyes zer | gling smad lo tsA'i khri dang gling stod phya pa'i khri yin zer yang | gling stod rgnog legs pa'i shes rab dang | gling smad du rngog blo ldan shes rab kyi khri yin la | gling stod smad du gyes pa'i rten 'brel yang byams 'jam dbyangs gnyis bgro gleng mdzad pa'i don gyis yin pas | grub mtha' smra ba gnyis 'byung bar lung bstan pas | sa dge gnyis dang | ra ba stod smad gnyis dang | gling stod smad ste gnyis mtshan gsum po de la 'gal ba med de | bstan pa dar ba la bzang 'gran du song ba yin no).

name: gsang phu mthong smon grwa tshang
gsang phu ne'u thog
alternative_names:
dpal ldan gsang phu ne'u thog
gsang phu sne'u thog
gsang ne'u thog
comments:
Upper part of the gSang valley (divided into gSang mda' and gSang phu).
In the 13th century, is part of the lay communities (mi sde) under the jurisdiction of the Tshal pa myriarchy ([CCSBSVCA]: 182 and n. 434).

See also: gsang phu gling smad, gsang phu gling stod, gsang phu ne'u thog gi gtsug lag khang

[9BEDJZXI]: 118 (gsang phu ne'u thog gi chos sde 'di ni | rngog legs pa'i shes rab kyis btab cing | jo bo a ti sha snye thang du bzhugs dus zhig der phyag 'dzub gtad nas | legs pa'i shes rab la lung pa ya gi'i phur dung g.yas 'khyil rang byon dang | nam mkha' gser gyi 'khor lo rtsibs stong dang ldan pa 'khor ba dang | thub dbang dpal du bzhugs nas | byams pa dang 'jam dbyangs sku gnyis dang gsum gyi | drang nges la bgro ba mdzad snang bas | der khyed kyis gnas gzhi byas na 'phrin las rgyas par 'gyur ro | zhes gsungs pas | 'o na re zhig gsang bar zhu zhes zhus pas gsang phur grags | kha cig ni lung pa gsang gi phu yin pas gsang phur brjod zer).

name: gsang phu ne'u thog gi gtsug lag khang
alternative_names:
gsang phu sne'u thog chos grwa chen po
gsang phu'i chos grwa
dpal ldan gsang phu ne'u thog gi gtsug lag khang
ne'u thog gi gtsug lag khang
gsang phu ne'u thog gi chos gra chen po
gsang phu ne'u thog gi chos gra
gsang phu ne'u thog dgon pa
chen po'i pho brang dge ba'i gnas
comments:
Part of the present-day sNe'u district, one of the xiang of sTod lung bDe chen county. ([CCSBSVCA]: 116, n. 92)
Founded in 1073 according to the <<Deb ther sngon po [R894SIKE]>> (392: dang por brag nag gi rtsar dgon pa zhig mdzad pa las phyis jo bos lung bstan pa bzhin chu mo glang gi lo la ne'u thog gi gtsug lag khang chen mo btab|; 490: mar pa'i drug cu rtsa gnyis pa chu mo glang la sa skya dang ne'u thog btab |) and the Yar lung chos 'byung ([CCSBSVCA]: 116, n. 92). Other dates of foundation: 1059 (the year of rNgog Blo ldan shes rab's birth), 1071 ([7FZ3B926]: 106; [MPRU5QUC]: 205]).
Said to be named after the "weasel" (ne'u le) which assisted during the foundation, which can be associated with the klu'i rgyal po Ne'u le Thod dkar.
Initially financially supported by the 'Bring tsho clan ([7FZ3B926]: 108), it comprised 500 pupils.
Its chief protector god is the bSe khrab can, considered a wrathful manifestation of Tshangs pa dkar po ([CCSBSVCA]: 247, n. 710).
It split into two branches in the first part of the 1100s, see (ID:6158) and (ID:6172).

On the abbatial succession, see [7FZ3B926], [MPRU5QUC], [CCSBSVCA]: V.8.1.

See also: gsang phu sne'u thog, gsang phu gling smad, gsang phu gling stod

The institution was famed for being a repository of the instruction of bka' gdams pa oral precepts (man ngag) ([2X2E3882]: 272, n. 169).

alternative_names: menri
comments: The first monastery at sMan ri was founded in 1072 and destroyed by a flood in 1386. A new monastery was erected in 1405.

name: stod lung sa thang
alternative_names:
stod lungs se thang
stod lungs sa thang
gad se thang
gad sa thang
comments:
[CCSBSVCA]: 420, n. 25 suggests that [Gad] Se/Sa thang, where he set up a "grwa gsar" could be located in sByar rags xiang of sTod lung with the coordinates indicated here.
The expression "stod lung/lungs yul thang" appears to refer to the very same place: a plain in the region of sTod lung.

name: tho ling
alternative_names:
thon mthing gi gtsug lag khang
mtho lding
tho gling
mtho lding gser gyi lha khang
mtho lding rdo rje gdan
mtho lding dgon pa
comments:
Founded in 996 ([5VEXCHQ3]: 14). Location of the great religious council of 1076 (tho ling chos 'khor) organized by rTse lde (r. 1057-1088).
Converted to dGe lugs in 1450 (BDRC).
Entities linked to "thon mthing gi gtsug lag khang" are listed in the relations, as Dan Martin suggests that "thon mthing" could be an alternative spelling of "mtho lding," namely Tho ling (https://tibeto-logic.blogspot.com/2022/03/consecration-rite-of-great-translator.html, accessed 20.3.2024).

name: zul phu
alternative_names: zur phu
comments:
Founded as a Vinaya seminar. Located in present-day Tshad-sna xiang of Chu shur rdzong, on the southern bank of the sKyid chu river ([W4T5ZMUP]: 38).
Had close teaching bonds with gNas rnying (ID:4341) through Rin chen dpal bzang po (ID:6714).
Donated to the Karmapa in 1545.
Renovated and reconverted into a dGe lugs site in ca. 1639 by dKon mchog chos 'phel (ID:6328). Went into decay in the late 17th c. (See [W4T5ZMUP]: 38-39).
